title: Calliandra magdalenae
taxonomy: Calliandra magdalenae is a species within the genus Calliandra, which belongs to the family Fabaceae. The genus Calliandra is a group of flowering plants commonly known as powderpuffs due to their showy, pom-pom-like flowers. Calliandra magdalenae is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The species is classified as a type of shrub or small tree, with a range of characteristics that distinguish it from other species within the genus. Its classification is part of the broader system of plant taxonomy, which groups organisms based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
